The most common switching systems are **single-pole single-throw (SPST)**, **single-pole double-throw (SPDT)**, and **double-pole double-throw (DPDT)**, but the question likely refers to the **two fundamental types**: **make-before-break (MBB)** and **break-before-make (BBM)**. However, if considering **basic mechanical configurations**, the answer simplifies to **two primary switching actions** (normally open/closed).
